question_text: A student measures the density of an irregular rock by water displacement. The rock has a mass of 150 g. When placed in a measuring cylinder, the water level rises from 40 cm³ to 100 cm³. What is the density of the rock in g/cm³?
explanation: The volume of the rock is found by water displacement: 100 cm³ − 40 cm³ = 60 cm³. Using density = mass ÷ volume: density = 150 ÷ 60 = 2.5 g/cm³.
